versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: 1 In that day you will say:
Although You were angry with me,
I will trust and not be afraid.
For the LORD GOD is my strength and my song,
and He also has become my salvation.”
3 With joy you will draw water from the springs of salvation, 4 and on that day you will say:
Make His works known among the peoples;
declare that His name is exalted.
5 Sing to the LORD, for He has done glorious things.
Let this be known in all the earth.
6 Cry out and sing, O citizen of Zion,
for great among you is the Holy One of Israel.”

context_lines: In **Isaiah 12**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Isaiah.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **Joyful Thanksgiving** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Isaiah 12** remains the first control on meaning.
